text:
Once upon a time, in a small town nestled between rolling hills, there lived a peculiar cat named Rufus. Rufus wasn't just any cat—each night, before midnight, he would glide across the moonlit garden, singing in a voice like silver bells.

One sunny afternoon, while enjoying a late snack of carrots fresh from the barn, Rufus heard laughter. Curious, he sneaked out under the fence.

There stood a charming old woman with twinkling eyes and a basket full of fluffy white ducks. "Oh," she said cheerfully. "Didn't mean to disturb you. Haven't I seen you somewhere? You seem interesting."

"I couldn't help but enjoy your ducks," said Rufus. "It's an unusual pair."

"I call them my moonlit muskrats," she chuckled. "See, they're not really muskrats. These are my pet geese. But it's as though they dance under the moon. Do you ever do that?"
